name: org.machanism.machai:bindex-core
description: Bindex Core is a Java 17 Maven library for describing, registering, persisting, retrieving, filtering, and semantically recommending Bindex metadata records. It provides the Bindex JSON schema model used to describe artifacts for automatic recognition and integration with GenAI engines, MongoDB-backed repository services for storing serialized Bindex documents with embedding vectors, semantic selection through natural-language prompts, and AI-facing function tools for library discovery, Bindex lookup, GraphQL-style response filtering, schema retrieval, prompt retrieval, and Bindex registration from local files or JSON objects. The library is intended for AI agents, build tools, and application assembly workflows that need to discover libraries from requirements, generate or validate bindex.json metadata, register descriptors, and retrieve concise metadata for integration planning. Important classes include org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools, org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.Picker, org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexRepository, org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.MongoBindexRepository, and org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexInfo. The artifact integrates with MongoDB as an external backing system and uses GenAI and embedding providers configured by the MachAI runtime.

usageContext: Generating and validating bindex.json metadata for Java and Maven artifacts
Registering library descriptors for AI-assisted discovery and integration
Recommending libraries from natural-language project requirements
Retrieving Bindex descriptors by coordinates, local files, or remote URLs
Filtering Bindex metadata with GraphQL-style selection expressions
Persisting and searching Bindex records with MongoDB vector search

constructors.description: Creates an AI-facing Bindex function tool set. Use this object when exposing Bindex discovery, retrieval, registration, schema, and prompt functions to a MachAI tool-calling workflow.

examples.description: Instantiates the tool set so a hosting AI runtime can expose its annotated methods to agents. The actual invocation normally happens through the MachAI tool infrastructure, which supplies the project directory and configurator.

constructors.description: Creates a MongoDB-backed repository for Bindex documents. The configurator supplies BINDEX_REPO_URL or related repository settings. The repository can be used directly or passed to org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.Picker.

examples.description: Creates the repository in a try-with-resources block and retrieves a Bindex descriptor by its unique id. The configurator must be provided by the surrounding MachAI or application runtime.

constructors.description: Creates a semantic Bindex picker backed by a repository and runtime configuration. The picker generates embeddings for saved Bindex entries and recommends matching entries from natural-language prompts.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexRepository repository = new org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.MongoBindexRepository(configurator);
org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.Picker picker = new org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.Picker(repository, configurator);
examples.description: Wires the semantic picker to a MongoDB-backed repository. Use the picker to save descriptors or recommend libraries from prompts.

constructors.description: Creates an empty summary object for Bindex recommendation or search results. Populate it with id, version, description, and score when building custom repository implementations or reports.

examples.description: Creates and fills a BindexInfo result object with fully explicit values suitable for returning from a custom search or recommendation flow.

features.name: getBindex
features.signature: public org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex getBindex(java.lang.String id, java.lang.String query, java.io.File projectDir, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator configurator) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Retrieves Bindex metadata for a library or project by a Bindex coordinate id or by a remote HTTP/HTTPS URL pointing to a bindex.json descriptor. An optional GraphQL-style query can reduce the returned payload to selected fields.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools tools = new org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools();
org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex bindex = tools.getBindex("org.machanism.machai:bindex-core:1.2.2-SNAPSHOT", "{ name version classification { domains languages } }", projectDir, configurator);
examples.description: Retrieves only the name, version, and selected classification fields for a registered Bindex descriptor. This is useful when an AI agent needs concise metadata for reasoning.

features.name: getRecommendedLibraries
features.signature: public java.util.Collection<org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexInfo> getRecommendedLibraries(java.lang.String prompt, double score, int vectorSearchLimits, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator configurator) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Recommends registered libraries from a natural-language prompt. The method classifies the requirement, obtains embeddings, performs repository vector search, and returns BindexInfo summaries whose score is at or above the requested threshold.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools tools = new org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools();
java.util.Collection<org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexInfo> matches = tools.getRecommendedLibraries("Need a Java library to register artifact metadata and recommend integrations using semantic search", 0.85d, 25, configurator);
examples.description: Asks the Bindex recommendation service for Java libraries related to metadata registration and semantic discovery, returning only sufficiently relevant results.

features.name: registerBindex
features.signature: public java.lang.String registerBindex(java.lang.String path, java.io.File projectDir, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator configurator) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Registers a Bindex descriptor from a file located in the current project directory. If no path is supplied by the tool caller, the intended default is bindex.json.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools tools = new org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools();
java.lang.String registeredId = tools.registerBindex("bindex.json", projectDir, configurator);
examples.description: Registers the project-local bindex.json descriptor after it has been generated and validated. The returned string identifies the registered repository entry.

features.name: registerBindexJson
features.description: Registers an in-memory Bindex object without first writing it to disk. This is useful for AI or build workflows that generate structured Bindex data programmatically.

examples.description: Shows the shape of an in-memory registration flow. In production, fill all required Bindex fields before invoking registration.

features.name: getBindexSchema
features.signature: public java.lang.String getBindexSchema(java.net.URI uri) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Loads the Bindex JSON Schema v2 resource as UTF-8 text from the classpath. AI agents and build tools can use this schema to validate generated bindex.json files.

examples.description: Loads the schema text so a caller can inspect required fields or validate generated descriptors before registration.

features.name: bindexGenerationPrompts
features.description: Loads the prompt template used for Bindex generation. This helps AI workflows generate descriptors consistently with the schema and project conventions.

examples.description: Retrieves the generation instructions before asking a model to create bindex.json metadata.

features.name: save
features.description: Saves a Bindex entry through org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.Picker, generating and storing its embedding vector for semantic search.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.Picker picker = new org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.Picker(repository, configurator);
java.lang.String savedId = picker.save(bindex);
examples.description: Persists a fully populated Bindex descriptor and its generated embedding so it can be found by later recommendation calls.

features.name: pick
features.signature: public java.util.Collection<org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexInfo> pick(java.lang.String prompt, long vectorSearchLimits, double score, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator configurator) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Recommends matching Bindex entries from a natural-language prompt using classification, embeddings, repository filtering, vector search limits, and a minimum relevance score.

examples.snippet: java.util.Collection<org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexInfo> results = picker.pick("Find Java infrastructure libraries for metadata registration and semantic discovery", 25L, 0.85d, configurator);
examples.description: Runs a practical recommendation query against the repository and returns concise BindexInfo summaries ordered by relevance.

customizations.name: MODEL_PROP_NAME
customizations.description: Configuration property name used by BindexFunctionTools to select the model used for AI-assisted operations.

examples.description: Reads the public constant so application configuration can refer to the same model property name as the tool implementation.

customizations.name: GraphQL-style field selection
customizations.description: The getBindex tool accepts GraphQL-style field selection strings to reduce returned Bindex JSON. This is useful for minimizing token usage in downstream language-model processing.

examples.snippet: java.lang.String query = "{ name version classification { languages domains targetEnvironment } }";
examples.description: Configures a narrow response shape containing only the metadata needed for classification and integration planning.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexRepository
studs.description: Repository boundary for Bindex record management. Implementations retrieve Bindex descriptors by id, save descriptors with embeddings, and find matching descriptors using classification filters and semantic vector similarity.

examples.description: Uses the repository interface while relying on the MongoDB-backed implementation at runtime.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools
studs.description: AI tool boundary implemented by org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools so its annotated functions can be exposed to MachAI-compatible agents.

examples.description: Treats the Bindex tool set as a generic MachAI function-tool provider.

examples.scenario: Install bindex-core as a Maven dependency
steps: Add the Maven dependency org.machanism.machai:bindex-core:1.2.2-SNAPSHOT to a Java 17 project.
Ensure the MachAI snapshot or internal repository that hosts the artifact is configured in Maven if the version is not available from your default repositories.
Use the classes under org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools and org.machanism.machai.bindex.core from application code or an AI tool runtime.
examples.description: This scenario installs the library in a Maven project so Bindex tools, schema POJOs, and repository APIs are available on the classpath.

examples.scenario: Configure and register a project-local bindex.json descriptor
steps: Create a valid bindex.json file in the project root directory.
Configure the runtime with repository settings such as BINDEX_REPO_URL or the equivalent configurator property expected by the application.
Instantiate org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools or expose it through the MachAI tool-calling infrastructure.
Call registerBindex with the path bindex.json and the current project directory.
Store the returned registration id for audit or update workflows.
examples.description: This practical workflow registers metadata generated for a Maven project so it can be searched and recommended by Bindex services.

steps: Configure MongoDB repository access and the GenAI or embedding providers required by the MachAI runtime.
Create org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools in the host application or invoke it as a tool from an AI agent.
Write a natural-language prompt describing required functionality, target language, architecture layer, and integration needs.
Set a minimum score such as 0.85 and a vector search limit such as 25.
Use returned org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexInfo entries to select candidate libraries, then call getBindex for full metadata if needed.
examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools tools = new org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools();
java.util.Collection<org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexInfo> libraries = tools.getRecommendedLibraries("Need a Java library for AI-assisted metadata registration, MongoDB-backed persistence, and semantic library discovery", 0.85d, 25, configurator);
examples.description: This scenario shows how to run a real-world library recommendation request and obtain concise result summaries for integration planning.

examples.scenario: Retrieve concise metadata with GraphQL-style filtering
steps: Choose the Bindex id or descriptor URL to inspect.
Create a GraphQL-style selection string containing only the fields needed by the caller.
Call getBindex with the id, query, project directory, and configurator.
Use the filtered response to reduce downstream token usage in AI workflows.
examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools tools = new org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools.BindexFunctionTools();
org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex descriptor = tools.getBindex("org.machanism.machai:bindex-core:1.2.2-SNAPSHOT", "{ name version description classification { domains languages targetEnvironment integrations } }", new java.io.File("."), configurator);
examples.description: This scenario demonstrates how an agent retrieves only classification-rich fields needed to decide whether the artifact fits a project.
